tree.setup: You sit in a calligrapher's studio in Hangzhou. Mr. Shen, ninety-one years old, is grinding ink on a stone his grandfather used. The room smells of pine soot and aged paper. He hasn't taken a student in fifteen years.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐在杭州一位书法家的工作室里。九十一岁的沈先生正在用他祖父用过的砚台磨墨。房间里弥漫着松烟和陈纸的气味。他已经十五年没有收过学生了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这块砚台是我祖父的，他祖父的，再往上我就不知道了。至少五代人磨过它。你看中间这个凹陷——这不是设计出来的，是一百多年的手磨出来的。每一代人的手都在同一个位置画圈，墨和水和石头之间发生了一种极其缓慢的对话。这个凹陷是对话的痕迹。有人问我为什么不用墨汁——因为墨汁是结果。磨墨是过程。书法的一半在你提笔之前就已经完成了——在你磨墨的那十五分钟里，你的呼吸慢下来了，手的温度传到了墨上，你和纸之间的关系在你还没碰到纸之前就已经开始建立了。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè kuài yàntái shì wǒ zǔfù de, tā zǔfù de, zài wǎng shàng wǒ jiù bù zhīdào le. Zhìshǎo wǔ dài rén mó guò tā. Nǐ kàn zhōngjiān zhège āoxiàn — zhè bú shì shèjì chūlái de, shì yībǎi duō nián de shǒu mó chūlái de. Měi yī dài rén de shǒu dōu zài tóng yī ge wèizhì huà quān, mò hé shuǐ hé shítou zhī jiān fāshēng le yī zhǒng jíqí huǎnmàn de duìhuà. Zhège āoxiàn shì duìhuà de hénjì. Yǒu rén wèn wǒ wèishéme bú yòng mòzhī — yīnwèi mòzhī shì jiéguǒ. Mó mò shì guòchéng. Shūfǎ de yībàn zài nǐ tí bǐ zhīqián jiù yǐjīng wánchéng le — zài nǐ mó mò de nà shíwǔ fēnzhōng lǐ, nǐ de hūxī màn xiàlái le, shǒu de wēndù chuán dào le mò shàng, nǐ hé zhǐ zhī jiān de guānxi zài nǐ hái méi pèng dào zhǐ zhīqián jiù yǐjīng kāishǐ jiànlì le.
turns.text_en: This inkstone was my grandfather's, and his grandfather's — beyond that I don't know. At least five generations have ground ink on it. See this hollow in the center — it wasn't designed, it was worn by over a hundred years of hands. Every generation's hand circled the same spot, and between ink and water and stone a supremely slow conversation took place.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea that half of calligraphy happens before the brush touches paper.

options.text_zh: 磨墨不是在等书法开始——磨墨就是书法。呼吸变慢、手的温度传递——这不是前奏，是第一乐章。那些看似什么都没发生的时刻，其实一切都在发生。
options.text_pinyin: Mó mò bú shì zài děng shūfǎ kāishǐ — mó mò jiù shì shūfǎ. Hūxī biàn màn, shǒu de wēndù chuándì — zhè bú shì qiánzòu, shì dì yī yuèzhāng. Nàxiē kàn sì shénme dōu méi fāshēng de shíkè, qíshí yīqiè dōu zài fāshēng.
options.text_en: Grinding ink isn't waiting for calligraphy to start — grinding is calligraphy. Breathing slowing, warmth transferring — that's not a prelude, it's the first movement. Those moments when seemingly nothing is happening — in fact, everything is happening.
options.feedback: Mr. Shen stops grinding and looks up. He studies you for a moment, then sets the ink stick down and gestures for you to come closer to the stone.

options.text_pinyin: Nín měi tiān xiě duōshǎo ge zì?
options.text_en: How many characters do you write each day?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen glances up. 'Some days one. Some days none.' He returns to grinding. The question landed in the wrong dimension.

options.text_zh: 这块砚台现在值多少钱？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè kuài yàntái xiànzài zhí duōshǎo qián?
options.text_en: What would this inkstone be worth now?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen keeps grinding without looking up. After a moment he says, 'I have no idea.' His tone makes clear he's never needed to know.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 第一乐章——这个比喻很精确。但我还要告诉你一件事：墨磨出来以后，它在砚台上的状态只能维持很短的时间。磨得太早，墨干了。磨得太晚，心还没准备好。最好的墨是在你刚好准备好的那一刻磨出来的——你的心、你的墨、你的纸同时到达了那个状态。这种同时性不能计划——它只能被等待。我年轻的时候总是急着写。现在我明白了：不是我在等墨准备好——是墨在等我准备好。而大多数时候，我还没准备好的原因不是技术不够，是心里还有噪音。
turns.text_pinyin: Dì yī yuèzhāng — zhège bǐyù hěn jīngquè. Dàn wǒ hái yào gàosu nǐ yī jiàn shì: mò mó chūlái yǐhòu, tā zài yàntái shàng de zhuàngtài zhǐ néng wéichí hěn duǎn de shíjiān. Mó de tài zǎo, mò gān le. Mó de tài wǎn, xīn hái méi zhǔnbèi hǎo. Zuì hǎo de mò shì zài nǐ gānghǎo zhǔnbèi hǎo de nà yī kè mó chūlái de — nǐ de xīn, nǐ de mò, nǐ de zhǐ tóngshí dàodá le nàge zhuàngtài. Zhè zhǒng tóngshíxìng bù néng jìhuà — tā zhǐ néng bèi děngdài. Wǒ niánqīng de shíhou zǒng shì jí zhe xiě. Xiànzài wǒ míngbai le: bú shì wǒ zài děng mò zhǔnbèi hǎo — shì mò zài děng wǒ zhǔnbèi hǎo. Ér dàduōshù shíhou, wǒ hái méi zhǔnbèi hǎo de yuányīn bú shì jìshù bú gòu, shì xīnlǐ hái yǒu zàoyīn.
turns.text_en: First movement — that metaphor is precise. But I need to tell you something else: after ink is ground, its state on the stone only lasts a short time. Ground too early, the ink dries. Ground too late, the heart isn't ready. The best ink is ground at the exact moment you happen to be ready — your heart, your ink, your paper all arrive at that state simultaneously.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea that readiness is about inner silence, not technique.

options.text_zh: 技术是加法——一笔、两笔、一万笔。安静是减法——放下焦虑、比较、对结果的期待。当减法减到最后，剩下的只有你和墨和纸。那个时刻，字自己就写出来了。
options.text_pinyin: Jìshù shì jiāfǎ — yī bǐ, liǎng bǐ, yī wàn bǐ. Ānjìng shì jiǎnfǎ — fàng xià jiāolǜ, bǐjiào, duì jiéguǒ de qīdài. Dāng jiǎnfǎ jiǎn dào zuìhòu, shèng xià de zhǐyǒu nǐ hé mò hé zhǐ. Nà ge shíkè, zì zìjǐ jiù xiě chūlái le.
options.text_en: Technique is addition — one stroke, two strokes, ten thousand strokes. Silence is subtraction — setting down anxiety, comparison, expectation of results. When subtraction is complete, what remains is only you and ink and paper.
options.feedback: Mr. Shen looks at you steadily. Then he picks up the brush, loads it with ink, and places a blank sheet in front of you. He doesn't speak.

options.text_zh: 您是怎么让自己安静下来的？
options.text_pinyin: Nín shì zěnme ràng zìjǐ ānjìng xiàlái de?
options.text_en: How do you make yourself become quiet?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen looks at the inkstone. 'I grind,' he says. 'For as long as it takes.' He goes back to circling. He's given you the answer but you've asked for a method instead of hearing it.

options.text_pinyin: Míngxiǎng duì shūfǎ yǒu bāngzhù ma?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen considers this. 'Grinding is meditation,' he says simply. He seems mildly puzzled that you're looking for something separate.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 减法——是的。你理解了。我花了大半辈子才学会做减法。年轻时候的字，每一笔都在喊「看我」。后来我开始删：删掉了炫技，删掉了想要被认可的欲望，删掉了「我在写字」这个意识本身。最好的字是写完以后你甚至不记得自己写过的。不是因为不专注——恰恰相反——是因为太专注了，专注到「我」消失了。剩下的只有墨在纸上的运动。这不是技术的巅峰——是自我的消融。我现在九十一岁了。我写字写了八十年。八十年学会的不是怎么写——是怎么消失在写的过程里。
turns.text_pinyin: Jiǎnfǎ — shì de. Nǐ lǐjiě le. Wǒ huā le dàbàn bèizi cái xuéhuì zuò jiǎnfǎ. Niánqīng shíhou de zì, měi yī bǐ dōu zài hǎn 'kàn wǒ'. Hòulái wǒ kāishǐ shān: shān diào le xuànjì, shān diào le xiǎng yào bèi rènkě de yùwàng, shān diào le 'wǒ zài xiě zì' zhège yìshí běnshēn. Zuì hǎo de zì shì xiě wán yǐhòu nǐ shènzhì bú jìde zìjǐ xiě guò de. Bú shì yīnwèi bù zhuānzhù — qiàqià xiāngfǎn — shì yīnwèi tài zhuānzhù le, zhuānzhù dào 'wǒ' xiāoshī le. Shèng xià de zhǐyǒu mò zài zhǐ shàng de yùndòng. Zhè bú shì jìshù de diānfēng — shì zìwǒ de xiāoróng. Wǒ xiànzài jiǔshíyī suì le. Wǒ xiě zì xiě le bāshí nián. Bāshí nián xuéhuì de bú shì zěnme xiě — shì zěnme xiāoshī zài xiě de guòchéng lǐ.
turns.text_en: Subtraction — yes. You understand. I spent most of my life learning subtraction. The characters of my youth — every stroke shouted 'look at me.' Later I began to delete: deleted the showing off, deleted the desire to be recognized, deleted the consciousness of 'I am writing' itself. The best characters are ones you don't even remember writing afterward.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to eighty years of learning to disappear into the process.

options.text_zh: 您花了一辈子学习如何不存在于您做的事情里——但恰恰是这种不存在让字活了起来。当「我」消失了，字才自由了，不再为您服务，为自己而存在。创作者消失，作品才真正诞生。
options.text_pinyin: Nín huā le yī bèizi xuéxí rúhé bù cúnzài yú nín zuò de shìqíng lǐ — dàn qiàqià shì zhè zhǒng bù cúnzài ràng zì huó le qǐlái. Dāng 'wǒ' xiāoshī le, zì cái zìyóu le, bú zài wèi nín fúwù, wèi zìjǐ ér cúnzài. Chuàngzuòzhě xiāoshī, zuòpǐn cái zhēnzhèng dànshēng.
options.text_en: You spent a lifetime learning how not to exist in what you do — and precisely that absence is what makes the characters alive.
options.feedback: Mr. Shen is still for a moment. Then he picks up the brush himself, very slowly, and writes one character. He sets the brush down and turns the paper toward you.

options.text_pinyin: Nín de zuòpǐn yǒu méiyǒu mài guò?
options.text_en: Have you ever sold your work?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen nods once. 'When I was young, yes.' He says nothing more. The question landed somewhere far from where the conversation was going.

options.text_zh: 这跟道家说的「无为」是一个意思吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè gēn Dàojiā shuō de 'wúwéi' shì yī ge yìsi ma?
options.text_en: Is this the same as the Daoist idea of wuwei?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen tilts his head. 'You can call it that,' he says mildly. He picks up the ink stick again. He'd rather you stayed with the experience than named it.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 创作者消失，作品才诞生——你说到了我用八十年才到达的地方。最后让我给你看一样东西。这是我昨天写的一个字：「空」。你看——这个字里什么也没有。但什么也没有，恰恰是它要说的全部。我写它的时候不在想任何事情。我不知道我要写什么字，但手动了，墨落了，「空」出现了。这个字不是我选的——是墨和纸和我的手共同决定的。也许这就是写了八十年以后唯一能写出来的字——一个关于「什么都没有」的字。但你看它——它空吗？它比任何字都满。
turns.text_pinyin: Chuàngzuòzhě xiāoshī, zuòpǐn cái dànshēng — nǐ shuō dào le wǒ yòng bāshí nián cái dàodá de dìfang. Zuìhòu ràng wǒ gěi nǐ kàn yī yàng dōngxi. Zhè shì wǒ zuótiān xiě de yī ge zì: 'kōng'. Nǐ kàn — zhège zì lǐ shénme yě méiyǒu. Dàn shénme yě méiyǒu, qiàqià shì tā yào shuō de quánbù. Wǒ xiě tā de shíhou bú zài xiǎng rènhé shìqíng. Wǒ bù zhīdào wǒ yào xiě shénme zì, dàn shǒu dòng le, mò luò le, 'kōng' chūxiàn le. Zhège zì bú shì wǒ xuǎn de — shì mò hé zhǐ hé wǒ de shǒu gòngtóng juédìng de. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì xiě le bāshí nián yǐhòu wéiyī néng xiě chūlái de zì — yī ge guānyú 'shénme dōu méiyǒu' de zì. Dàn nǐ kàn tā — tā kōng ma? Tā bǐ rènhé zì dōu mǎn.
turns.text_en: The creator disappears, the work is born — you've arrived at a place it took me eighty years to reach. Let me show you one last thing. This is a character I wrote yesterday: 'empty' (空). Look — there's nothing in this character. But nothing is precisely all it has to say. When I wrote it, I wasn't thinking about anything.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the character 'empty' that is fuller than any other.

options.text_zh: 它空，因为写的时候什么都没想。它满，因为写之前的八十年什么都经历过了、放下了。这不是起点的空——是终点的空。就像砚台上那个凹陷，看着是空的，里面装了五代人的时间。
options.text_pinyin: Tā kōng, yīnwèi xiě de shíhou shénme dōu méi xiǎng. Tā mǎn, yīnwèi xiě zhīqián de bāshí nián shénme dōu jīnglì guò le, fàng xià le. Zhè bú shì qǐdiǎn de kōng — shì zhōngdiǎn de kōng. Jiù xiàng yàntái shàng nàge āoxiàn, kàn zhe shì kōng de, lǐmiàn zhuāng le wǔ dài rén de shíjiān.
options.text_en: It's empty because you thought of nothing while writing. It's full because in the eighty years before, you experienced everything and set it all down.
options.feedback: Mr. Shen places the paper in front of you and folds his hands. He says nothing — but he doesn't look away. The lesson is over and also just beginning.

options.text_pinyin: Wǒ kěyǐ pāi yī zhāng zhàopiàn ma?
options.text_en: May I take a photo of it?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen's expression softens almost imperceptibly. 'If you like,' he says. But the moment shifts — you've moved from looking at the character to wanting to keep it, which is a different kind of looking.

options.text_en: What script style is this written in?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen names the style briefly and correctly. Then he waits. He's patient — but something in him quietly closes, like a door left slightly ajar that swings back.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese calligraphy (书法) is considered the highest visual art form in Chinese culture. The Four Treasures of the Study (文房四宝) — brush, ink, paper, and inkstone — are treated as partners rather than tools. The practice of grinding ink (磨墨) is considered meditative preparation, not mere chore. The concept of the artist's ego dissolving into the work connects to both Daoist wu-wei (无为) and Chan Buddhist non-attachment, but in calligraphy it is experienced directly through the body rather than theorized abstractly.
